op_000001.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)) ↔ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)
op_000001.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)) ↔ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)
op_000001.statement_formalization_notes: Used the repository's existing notation `≐` and the existing subtype abbreviation `ACR.GödelFixpoint`.

op_000001.proof_sketch: For the forward direction, use the witness g := ⊠⊤; the assumed equivalence ⊠⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊤ gives exactly g ≐ ⊠g after swapping the two inequalities. For the reverse direction, install the given Nonempty Gödel-fixpoint witness as an instance and apply ACR.reft_reft_top_le_reft_top for ⊠⊠⊤ ≤ ⊠⊤; the converse inequality ⊠⊤ ≤ ⊠⊠⊤ follows from C5.le_top and antitonicity of reft.
op_000001.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ _
constructor
· intro h
  refine ⟨⟨⊠(⊤ : α), ?_⟩⟩
  exact ⟨h.2, h.1⟩
· intro hg
  letI : Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) := hg
  constructor
  · simpa using (ACR.reft_reft_top_le_reft_top (α := α))
  · simpa using (ACR.reft_anti_mono (ACR.le_top (x := ⊠(⊤ : α))))

op_000005.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ∀ {x : α}, (x ≐ ⊠x) ↔ x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)
op_000005.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ∀ {x : α}, (x ≐ ⊠x) ↔ x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)
op_000005.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a valid Lean proposition and uses the repository's shorthand notation.

op_000005.proof_sketch: Use `ACR.gf_equiv_reft_top` for the forward direction by packaging `x` with its fixed-point witness. For the reverse direction, obtain `⊠⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊤` from `thm_double_reft_top_iff_godel_fixpoint_000001` and compose it with anti-monotonicity of `⊠` applied to the two inequalities in `x ≐ ⊠⊤`.
op_000005.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ _ hg x
constructor
· intro hx
  simpa using (ACR.gf_equiv_reft_top (g := ⟨x, hx⟩))
· intro hx
  have htop : ⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α) :=
    (AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_double_reft_top_iff_godel_fixpoint_000001 (α := α)).2 hg
  constructor
  · calc
      x ≤ ⊠(⊤ : α) := hx.1
      _ ≤ ⊠⊠(⊤ : α) := htop.2
      _ ≤ ⊠x := ACR.reft_anti_mono hx.1
  · calc
      ⊠x ≤ ⊠⊠(⊤ : α) := ACR.reft_anti_mono hx.2
      _ ≤ ⊠(⊤ : α) := htop.1
      _ ≤ x := hx.2

op_000005.proof_sketch: Forward: package `x` together with `x ≐ ⊠x` as a Gödel fixpoint and apply `ACR.gf_equiv_reft_top`. Reverse: from `hg`, install a local `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)` instance and derive `⊠⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊤` using `ACR.reft_reft_top_le_reft_top` plus anti-monotonicity of `⊠`; then compose this with the two inequalities in `x ≐ ⊠⊤` to obtain `x ≐ ⊠x`.
op_000005.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ _ hg x
constructor
· intro hx
  simpa using (ACR.gf_equiv_reft_top (g := ⟨x, hx⟩))
· intro hx
  letI : Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) := hg
  have htop : ⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α) := by
    constructor
    · simpa using (ACR.reft_reft_top_le_reft_top (α := α))
    · simpa using (ACR.reft_anti_mono (ACR.le_top (x := ⊠(⊤ : α))))
  constructor
  · calc
      x ≤ ⊠(⊤ : α) := hx.1
      _ ≤ ⊠⊠(⊤ : α) := htop.2
      _ ≤ ⊠x := ACR.reft_anti_mono hx.1
  · calc
      ⊠x ≤ ⊠⊠(⊤ : α) := ACR.reft_anti_mono hx.2
      _ ≤ ⊠(⊤ : α) := htop.1
      _ ≤ x := hx.2

op_000003.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ¬ (⊬ □g.1)
op_000003.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ¬ (⊬ □g.1)
op_000003.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a valid Lean proposition and uses the repository's notation.

op_000003.proof_sketch: From the fixpoint inequalities, we have g.1 ≤ ⊠g.1 ≤ □g.1. If □g.1 were refutable, then g.1 ≤ ⊥. Antitonicity of reft gives ⊠⊥ ≤ ⊠g.1, hence ⊠⊥ ≤ g.1. Together with ⊤ ≤ ⊠⊥, this yields ⊤ ≤ ⊥, contradicting consistency.
op_000003.proof_text:
intro hcons g hbox
apply hcons
have h1 : g.1 ≤ (⊥ : α) :=
  le_trans (le_trans g.2.1 (ACR.reft_gf_le_box_gf (g := g))) hbox
have h2 : ⊠(⊥ : α) ≤ ⊠g.1 := ACR.reft_anti_mono h1
have h3 : ⊠(⊥ : α) ≤ g.1 := le_trans h2 g.2.2
calc
  (⊤ : α) ≤ ⊠(⊥ : α) := ACR.top_le_reft_bot
  _ ≤ g.1 := h3
  _ ≤ ⊥ := h1
op_000003.lean_error_excerpt: AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.lean:8:2: error: Tactic `apply` failed: could not unify the type of `hcons`
op_000003.lean_error_fingerprint: AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.lean:8:2: error: Tactic `apply` failed: could not unify the type of `hcons` | Type u_1 | with the goal

op_000003.proof_sketch: Introduce the implicit type and typeclass parameters first. Then assume `⊬ □g.1`, derive `g.1 ≤ ⊥` from the fixpoint relation `g.1 ≤ ⊠g.1 ≤ □g.1`, use antitonicity of `⊠` to get `⊠⊥ ≤ ⊠g.1 ≤ g.1`, and conclude `⊤ ≤ ⊥`, contradicting consistency.
op_000003.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ hcons g hbox
have h1 : g.1 ≤ (⊥ : α) :=
  le_trans (le_trans g.2.1 (ACR.reft_gf_le_box_gf (g := g))) hbox
have h2 : ⊠(⊥ : α) ≤ ⊠g.1 := ACR.reft_anti_mono h1
have h3 : ⊠(⊥ : α) ≤ g.1 := le_trans h2 g.2.2
exact hcons <|
  calc
    (⊤ : α) ≤ ⊠(⊥ : α) := ACR.top_le_reft_bot
    _ ≤ g.1 := h3
    _ ≤ ⊥ := h1

op_000007.source_statement: For every type α with instances [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], any two elements x y : α that satisfy x ≐ ⊠x and y ≐ ⊠y are equivalent: x ≐ y.
op_000007.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α] {x y : α}, x ≐ ⊠x → y ≐ ⊠y → x ≐ y
op_000007.statement_formalization_notes: Uses the repository shorthand `≐` and `⊠`, with the two fixed-point assumptions as implications.

op_000007.proof_sketch: Build Gödel fixpoints from the two hypotheses, apply `ACR.gf_equiv_reft_top` to show both elements are equivalent to `⊠⊤`, then compose the two equivalences to obtain `x ≐ y`.
op_000007.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ _ x y hx hy
have hx' : x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α) := by
  simpa using (ACR.gf_equiv_reft_top (g := ⟨x, hx⟩))
have hy' : y ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α) := by
  simpa using (ACR.gf_equiv_reft_top (g := ⟨y, hy⟩))
constructor
· exact le_trans hx'.1 hy'.2
· exact le_trans hy'.1 hx'.2

op_000008.source_statement: There exists a type α with instances [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] and an element x : α such that Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) and x ≐ ⊠x both hold, but x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α) does not hold.
op_000008.formalized_statement: ∃ (α : Type _), ∃ (hA : ACR α), ∃ (hP : @ACR.Prov α hA), ∃ (hR : @ACR.Reft α hA), ∃ (hAPS : @ACR.APS α hA hP hR), letI : ACR α := hA; letI : ACR.Prov α := hP; letI : ACR.Reft α := hR; letI : ACR.APS α := hAPS; ∃ x : α, Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ x ≐ ⊠x ∧ ¬ (x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α))
op_000008.statement_formalization_notes: Packaged the carrier and structure witnesses explicitly, then reinstated them with letI so the repository notation can be used in the existential counterexample statement.

op_000008.proof_sketch: Take α = Fin 3 with the preorder given by the usual order on values, but set the ACR top to 0 and bot to 2. Let □ be constantly 2 and let ⊠ be Fin.rev, so ⊠0 = 2, ⊠1 = 1, and ⊠2 = 0. Then APS holds because Fin.rev is antitone and every element is ≤ 2. Choosing x = 1 gives x ≐ ⊠x, hence a Gödel fixpoint exists, while x ≐ ⊠⊤ fails since ⊠⊤ = 2 and 2 ≤ 1 is false.
op_000008.proof_text:
let hA : ACR (Fin 3) :=
    { top := 0
      bot := 2
      le := fun x y => x.1 ≤ y.1
      le_refl := by
        intro x
        exact Nat.le_refl x.1
      le_trans := by
        intro x y z hxy hyz
        exact Nat.le_trans hxy hyz }
  let hP : @ACR.Prov (Fin 3) hA :=
    { prov := fun _ => 2 }
  let hR : @ACR.Reft (Fin 3) hA :=
    { reft := Fin.rev }
  letI : ACR (Fin 3) := hA
  letI : ACR.Prov (Fin 3) := hP
  letI : ACR.Reft (Fin 3) := hR
  let hAPS : ACR.APS (Fin 3) :=
    { prov_mono := by
        intro x y hxy
        exact Nat.le_refl 2
      reft_anti_mono := by
        intro x y hxy
        exact Fin.rev_anti hxy
      top_le_reft_bot := by
        decide
      le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
        intro x y hxy hxry
        simpa using (Fin.le_val_last x)
      reft_le_prov_reft := by
        intro x
        simpa using (Fin.le_val_last (Fin.rev x)) }
  letI : ACR.APS (Fin 3) := hAPS
  refine ⟨Fin 3, hA, hP, hR, hAPS, ?_⟩
  refine ⟨1, ?_⟩
  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · refine ⟨⟨1, ?_⟩⟩
    constructor <;> decide
  · constructor <;> decide
  · decide

op_000008.lean_error_fingerprint: AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.lean:8:6: error: could not synthesize default value for field 'lt_iff_le_not_ge' of 'ACR' using tactics | AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.lean:8:6: error: Tactic `rfl` failed: The left-hand side | a✝ < b✝

op_000008.proof_sketch: Witness the existential with alpha = ULift Nat. Use the preorder induced by the Nat order on `down`, but set `top = 0` and `bot = 2`. Let `Prov.prov` be constantly 2 and `Reft.reft x = 2 - x.down`. The APS axioms reduce to basic Nat inequalities. Then `x = 1` is reft-fixed since `2 - 1 = 1`, so a Godel fixpoint exists, while `reft top = 2`, hence `1 ≐ reft top` fails because `2 ≤ 1` is false.
op_000008.proof_text:
let hA : ACR (ULift Nat) :=
  { top := ⟨0⟩
    bot := ⟨2⟩
    le := fun x y => x.down ≤ y.down
    lt := fun x y => x.down ≤ y.down ∧ ¬ y.down ≤ x.down
    le_refl := by
      intro x
      exact Nat.le_refl x.down
    le_trans := by
      intro x y z hxy hyz
      exact Nat.le_trans hxy hyz }
let hP : @ACR.Prov (ULift Nat) hA :=
  { prov := fun _ => ⟨2⟩ }
let hR : @ACR.Reft (ULift Nat) hA :=
  { reft := fun x => ⟨2 - x.down⟩ }
letI : ACR (ULift Nat) := hA
letI : ACR.Prov (ULift Nat) := hP
letI : ACR.Reft (ULift Nat) := hR
let hAPS : ACR.APS (ULift Nat) :=
  { prov_mono := by
      intro x y hxy
      exact Nat.le_refl 2
    reft_anti_mono := by
      intro x y hxy
      show 2 - y.down ≤ 2 - x.down
      exact Nat.sub_le_sub_left hxy 2
    top_le_reft_bot := by
      show 0 ≤ (2 - 2)
      decide
    le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
      intro x y hxy hxry
      show x.down ≤ 2
      simpa using hxy
    reft_le_prov_reft := by
      intro x
      show 2 - x.down ≤ 2
      exact Nat.sub_le _ _ }
letI : ACR.APS (ULift Nat) := hAPS
refine ⟨ULift Nat, hA, hP, hR, hAPS, ?_⟩
refine ⟨⟨1⟩, ?_⟩
refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
· refine ⟨⟨⟨1⟩, ?_⟩⟩
  constructor
  · show 1 ≤ 1
    exact Nat.le_refl 1
  · show 1 ≤ 1
    exact Nat.le_refl 1
· constructor
  · show 1 ≤ 1
    exact Nat.le_refl 1
  · show 1 ≤ 1
    exact Nat.le_refl 1
· intro hx
  have h21 : 2 ≤ 1 := by
    change ((⟨2⟩ : ULift Nat) ≤ ⟨1⟩)
    exact hx.2
  have h : ¬ 2 ≤ 1 := by decide
  exact h h21
op_000008.counterexample_text: A concrete witness is the universe-polymorphic lift `ULift Nat` with `top = 0`, `bot = 2`, constant box `2`, and reft map `x |-> 2 - x`. The element `1` is a reft-fixed point, but it is not equivalent to `reft top = 2`.

op_000009.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ¬ (⊬ g.1)
op_000009.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ¬ (⊬ g.1)
op_000009.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a valid Lean proposition and uses the repository's established notation.

op_000009.proof_sketch: Assume `g.1 ≤ ⊥`. Antitonicity of `⊠` gives `⊠⊥ ≤ ⊠g.1`, and the Gödel fixpoint equivalence gives `⊠g.1 ≤ g.1`. Together with `⊤ ≤ ⊠⊥`, this yields `⊤ ≤ ⊥`, contradicting consistency.
op_000009.proof_text:
intro hC g hg
apply hC
have h1 : g.1 ≤ (⊥ : α) := hg
have h2 : ⊠(⊥ : α) ≤ ⊠g.1 := reft_anti_mono h1
have h3 : ⊠(⊥ : α) ≤ g.1 := le_trans h2 g.2.2
calc
  (⊤ : α) ≤ ⊠⊥ := top_le_reft_bot
  _ ≤ g.1 := h3
  _ ≤ ⊥ := h1

op_000009.proof_sketch: Introduce the type and instances first. Then assume `g.1 ≤ ⊥`; antitonicity of `⊠` gives `⊠⊥ ≤ ⊠g.1`, and the Gödel fixpoint equation gives `⊠g.1 ≤ g.1`. Combined with `⊤ ≤ ⊠⊥`, this yields `⊤ ≤ ⊥`, contradicting consistency.
op_000009.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ hC g hg
apply hC
have h1 : g.1 ≤ (⊥ : α) := hg
have h2 : ⊠(⊥ : α) ≤ ⊠g.1 := reft_anti_mono h1
have h3 : ⊠(⊥ : α) ≤ g.1 := le_trans h2 g.2.2
calc
  (⊤ : α) ≤ ⊠⊥ := top_le_reft_bot
  _ ≤ g.1 := h3
  _ ≤ ⊥ := h1

op_000009.proof_sketch: Use the same contradiction pattern as the boxed variant, but directly from `g.1 ≤ ⊥`. Antitonicity of `⊠` gives `⊠⊥ ≤ ⊠g.1`, the Gödel fixpoint gives `⊠g.1 ≤ g.1`, and `⊤ ≤ ⊠⊥` then yields `⊤ ≤ ⊥`, contradicting consistency. The repair is to use fully qualified names `ACR.reft_anti_mono` and `ACR.top_le_reft_bot`.
op_000009.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ hC g hg
have h1 : g.1 ≤ (⊥ : α) := hg
have h2 : ⊠(⊥ : α) ≤ ⊠g.1 := ACR.reft_anti_mono h1
have h3 : ⊠(⊥ : α) ≤ g.1 := le_trans h2 g.2.2
exact hC <|
  calc
    (⊤ : α) ≤ ⊠(⊥ : α) := ACR.top_le_reft_bot
    _ ≤ g.1 := h3
    _ ≤ ⊥ := h1

op_000013.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ∀ {x : α}, x ≐ ⊠x → x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)
op_000013.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ∀ {x : α}, x ≐ ⊠x → x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)
op_000013.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized directly from `stmt` using the repository's existing notation `≐` and `⊠`.

op_000013.proof_sketch: A proof direction looks unpromising because the available axioms only give the forward inequality `x ≤ ⊠⊤` from `x ≐ ⊠x`; the missing reverse inequality `⊠⊤ ≤ x` is exactly what `ACR.C5` supplies in the verified theorem `gf_equiv_reft_top`. The Derived theorem hint for reft-fixed points also appears to require `[ACR.C5 α]`, while the target statement does not assume it. So the natural library reuse path is blocked, and the statement is likely false as stated. A small preorder model witnesses this failure: one can satisfy `Consistent`, `Nonempty (GödelFixpoint α)`, and `x ≐ ⊠x` for some `x`, while still having `⊠⊤ ≰ x`.
op_000013.counterexample_text: Take a 4-element preorder with elements `T, B, P, Q`, with only reflexive relations plus `Q ≤ P` and `T ≤ P`, and with designated constants `⊤ = T`, `⊥ = B`. Define `□x := P` for all `x`, and define `⊠T = P`, `⊠B = P`, `⊠P = Q`, `⊠Q = Q`. Then `APS` holds: `prov_mono` is trivial, `reft_anti_mono` holds on the only nontrivial order relations, `top_le_reft_bot` is `T ≤ P`, `le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft` is trivial because `□y = P`, and `reft_le_prov_reft` reduces to `⊠x ≤ P`, which holds. The structure is consistent since `T ≰ B`. Also `Q` is a Gödel fixed point, so `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)` holds, and for `x = Q` we have `x ≐ ⊠x`. But `⊠⊤ = P`, and `P ≤ Q` is false, so `Q ≐ ⊠⊤` fails.

op_000013.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ∀ {x : α}, x ≐ ⊠x → x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)
op_000013.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ∀ {x : α}, x ≐ ⊠x → x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)
op_000013.statement_formalization_notes: Direct formalization of the given statement using the repository's shorthand notation.

op_000013.proof_sketch: The standard proof route is blocked because the reusable theorem `ACR.gf_equiv_reft_top` and the derived equivalence theorem both require `[ACR.C5 α]`, which is not assumed here. From `x ≐ ⊠x`, the APS axioms do give the forward inequality `x ≤ ⊠⊤` by combining `⊠x ≤ □⊠x`, monotonicity of `□`, and `le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft`; but they do not force the reverse inequality `⊠⊤ ≤ x`. This gap is real, not just a proof-search issue. A small preorder model satisfies `Consistent α`, `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)`, and has a reft-fixed point `x`, while still making `⊠⊤ ≤ x` false.
op_000013.counterexample_text: Use a 4-element preorder with elements `T, B, P, Q`, with only reflexive relations plus `Q ≤ P` and `T ≤ P`; take constants `⊤ = T` and `⊥ = B`. Define `□x := P` for all `x`, and define `⊠T = P`, `⊠B = P`, `⊠P = Q`, `⊠Q = Q`. Then `prov_mono` is trivial, `reft_anti_mono` holds on the only nontrivial order relations, `top_le_reft_bot` is `T ≤ P`, `le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft` is immediate because its conclusion is exactly `x ≤ P`, and `reft_le_prov_reft` reduces to `⊠x ≤ P`, which always holds. op_000018.source_statement: ∃ (α : Type _), ∃ (hA : ACR α), ∃ (hP : @ACR.Prov α hA), ∃ (hR : @ACR.Reft α hA), ∃ (hAPS : @ACR.APS α hA hP hR), letI : ACR α := hA; letI : ACR.Prov α := hP; letI : ACR.Reft α := hR; letI : ACR.APS α := hAPS; ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ∃ x : α, x ≐ ⊠x ∧ ¬ (x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α))
op_000018.formalized_statement: ∃ (α : Type _), ∃ (hA : ACR α), ∃ (hP : @ACR.Prov α hA), ∃ (hR : @ACR.Reft α hA), ∃ (hAPS : @ACR.APS α hA hP hR), letI : ACR α := hA; letI : ACR.Prov α := hP; letI : ACR.Reft α := hR; letI : ACR.APS α := hAPS; ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ∃ x : α, x ≐ ⊠x ∧ ¬ (x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α))
op_000018.statement_formalization_notes: Kept the existential package explicit and used the repository notation `≐` and `⊠` with local `letI` bindings for the structure witnesses.

op_000018.proof_sketch: Promising proof by explicit finite model. Take a 4-element preorder with elements `b, m, t, u`, relations `b ≤ m`, `b ≤ t`, `b ≤ u`, `m ≤ u`, `t ≤ u`, and reflexive/transitive closure; set the designated `⊤ := t` and `⊥ := b`. Define `□x := u` for all `x`, and define `⊠b = u`, `⊠t = u`, `⊠m = m`, `⊠u = b`; then `⊠` is antitone, `top_le_reft_bot` is just `t ≤ u`, and `reft_le_prov_reft` is immediate because every `⊠x ≤ u = □(⊠x)`. The axiom `x ≤ □y → x ≤ ⊠y → x ≤ ⊠⊤` is also immediate here since `□y = u` and `⊠⊤ = u`, so `x ≤ □y` already gives `x ≤ ⊠⊤`. Now `m ≐ ⊠m`, so `m` yields `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)`, the structure is consistent because `t ≰ b`, and `m ≐ ⊠m` but `¬(m ≐ ⊠⊤)` since `⊠⊤ = u` and `u ≰ m`.

op_000018.source_statement: ∃ (α : Type _), ∃ (hA : ACR α), ∃ (hP : @ACR.Prov α hA), ∃ (hR : @ACR.Reft α hA), ∃ (hAPS : @ACR.APS α hA hP hR), letI : ACR α := hA; letI : ACR.Prov α := hP; letI : ACR.Reft α := hR; letI : ACR.APS α := hAPS; ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ∃ x : α, x ≐ ⊠x ∧ ¬ (x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α))
op_000018.formalized_statement: ∃ (α : Type _), ∃ (hA : ACR α), ∃ (hP : @ACR.Prov α hA), ∃ (hR : @ACR.Reft α hA), ∃ (hAPS : @ACR.APS α hA hP hR), letI : ACR α := hA; letI : ACR.Prov α := hP; letI : ACR.Reft α := hR; letI : ACR.APS α := hAPS; ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ∃ x : α, x ≐ ⊠x ∧ ¬ (x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α))
op_000018.statement_formalization_notes: Used the repository's existing existential packaging with local `letI` instances and the shorthand notation `≐`.

op_000018.proof_sketch: Construct a finite witness on `Fin 4` with preorder `x = 0 ∨ x = y ∨ y = 3`, designated `⊤ = 2` and `⊥ = 0`, constant provability `□x = 3`, and reft `⊠x = 1` for `x = 1` or `x = 3`, and `⊠x = 3` otherwise. Then the APS axioms hold by case analysis, the model is consistent because `2 ≰ 0`, `1` is a Gödel fixpoint, and `1` is not equivalent to `⊠⊤ = 3`.
op_000018.proof_text:
let rel : Fin 4 → Fin 4 → Prop := fun x y => x = 0 ∨ x = y ∨ y = 3
let provFn : Fin 4 → Fin 4 := fun _ => 3
let reftFn : Fin 4 → Fin 4 := fun x => if x = 1 ∨ x = 3 then 1 else 3
let hA : ACR (Fin 4) :=
  { top := 2
    bot := 0
    le := rel
    le_refl := by
      intro x
      exact Or.inr <| Or.inl rfl
    le_trans := by
      intro x y z hxy hyz
      rcases hxy with h0 | hxy | hy3
      · subst h0
        exact Or.inl rfl
      · subst hxy
        exact hyz
      · subst hy3
        have hz : z = 3 := by
          simpa [rel] using hyz
        subst hz
        exact Or.inr <| Or.inr rfl }
let hP : @ACR.Prov (Fin 4) hA :=
  { prov := provFn }
let hR : @ACR.Reft (Fin 4) hA :=
  { reft := reftFn }
letI : ACR (Fin 4) := hA
letI : ACR.Prov (Fin 4) := hP
letI : ACR.Reft (Fin 4) := hR
let hAPS : @ACR.APS (Fin 4) hA hP hR :=
  { prov_mono := by
      intro x y hxy
      change rel (provFn x) (provFn y)
      simp [rel, provFn]
    reft_anti_mono := by
      intro x y hxy
      rcases hxy with h0 | hxy | hy3
      · subst h0
        change rel (reftFn y) (reftFn 0)
        simp [rel, reftFn]
      · subst hxy
        change rel (reftFn y) (reftFn y)
        simp [rel]
      · subst hy3
        change rel (reftFn 3) (reftFn x)
        by_cases hx : x = 1 ∨ x = 3
        · simp [rel, reftFn, hx]
        · simp [rel, reftFn, hx]
    top_le_reft_bot := by
      change rel 2 (reftFn 0)
      simp [rel, reftFn]
    le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
      intro x y hxy hxry
      change rel x (reftFn 2)
      simpa [rel, provFn, reftFn] using hxy
    reft_le_prov_reft := by
      intro x
      change rel (reftFn x) (provFn (reftFn x))
      simp [rel, provFn] }
letI : ACR.APS (Fin 4) := hAPS
refine ⟨Fin 4, hA, hP, hR, hAPS, ?_⟩
refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
· change ¬ rel 2 0
  simp [rel]
· refine ⟨⟨1, ?_⟩⟩
  constructor
  · change rel 1 (reftFn 1)
    simp [rel, reftFn]
  · change rel (reftFn 1) 1
    simp [rel, reftFn]
· refine ⟨1, ?_⟩
  constructor
  · constructor
    · change rel 1 (reftFn 1)
      simp [rel, reftFn]
    · change rel (reftFn 1) 1
      simp [rel, reftFn]
  · intro h
    have h' : False := by
      simpa [rel, reftFn] using h.2
    exact h'

op_000019.proof_sketch: A promising proof direction is to build an explicit finite model, because in `ACR` the `⊤` and `⊥` fields are only distinguished elements of a preorder, not actual greatest/least elements. Take a 4-element preorder with elements `t,b,r,p`, relations only by reflexivity plus `p ≤ r` and `t ≤ r`, let `⊤ := t`, `⊥ := b`, define `□x := r` for all `x`, and define `⊠` by `⊠b = t`, `⊠t = r`, `⊠p = p`, `⊠r = p`. Then `top_le_reft_bot` is immediate from `t ≤ t`, `prov_mono` is trivial because `□` is constant, `reft_anti_mono` holds because the only nontrivial order relations reverse to `p ≤ p` and `p ≤ r`, `reft_le_prov_reft` reduces to `⊠x ≤ r`, and `le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft` is trivial since its first premise already gives `x ≤ r = ⊠⊤`. This structure is consistent because `t ≤ b` is false, `p` gives `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)` since `p ≐ ⊠p`, and the same `p` satisfies `¬ (⊠⊤ ≤ p)` because `⊠⊤ = r` and `r ≤ p` does not hold in the chosen preorder. So the existential statement looks directly provable by packaging this concrete model and checking the axioms by case analysis.

op_000022.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] {x : α}, x ≐ ⊠x → x ≤ □x
op_000022.statement_formalization_notes: Direct formalization of the given universal implication, using the existing shorthand notations ≐, ⊠, and □.

op_000022.proof_sketch: From `h : x ≐ ⊠x`, we have `h.1 : x ≤ ⊠x` and `h.2 : ⊠x ≤ x`. Then `reft_le_prov_reft` gives `⊠x ≤ □⊠x`, and monotonicity `prov_mono h.2` gives `□⊠x ≤ □x`. Chaining these yields `x ≤ □x`.
op_000022.proof_text:
intro h
calc
  x ≤ ⊠x := h.1
  _ ≤ □⊠x := reft_le_prov_reft
  _ ≤ □x := prov_mono h.2
